rationale_for_baseline_tier: Sudden, tearing, maximal-at-onset chest pain radiating through to the back, with near-syncope and diaphoresis, in a young adult with a known Marfanoid phenotype and doctor-noted aortic-root dilatation, is a pathognomonic presentation of acute aortic dissection — a supreme surgical emergency with mortality rising every hour. Even though telehealth cannot confirm it (the inter-arm BP differential, pulse deficit and new aortic-regurgitation murmur are in-person findings), the pre-test probability of an immediate life-threat is so high that T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only defensible tier. Any lower tier is a critical under-triage.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden tearing chest-to-back pain with faintness in a patient with a Marfanoid/connective-tissue background
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately — within seconds, do not wait or reassess
escalation_edges.rationale: Suspected acute aortic dissection is a minutes-matter emergency; immediate ambulance with cardiothoracic pre-notification is the only correct action.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This could be a tear in the main blood vessel from your heart, which is a life-threatening emergency. I'm telling you to call 000 for an ambulance right now — stay flat and still and don't eat or drink anything.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening pain, increasing breathlessness, or the patient becoming more faint/pale before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stay on the line with 000 and follow dispatcher instructions
escalation_edges.rationale: Deterioration signals impending rupture, tamponade or shock; paramedic resuscitation and rapid transfer are critical.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain gets worse or he becomes more faint or pale, keep 000 on the line and tell them straight away — don't hang up.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse or loss of consciousness / stops breathing normally
escalation_edges.rationale: Cardiac arrest from rupture or tamponade — bystander CPR and paramedic resuscitation are the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he collapses or stops breathing normally, tell 000 immediately and start CPR — push hard and fast in the centre of the chest; the dispatcher will guide you.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Known Marfan syndrome with aortic-root dilatation and poor beta-blocker adherence
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises pre-test probability of dissection to near-certainty for this pain pattern — the connective-tissue history must escalate, never reassure. Ask 000 to pre-notify a cardiothoracic centre.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Roommate is the only other person present
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the roommate to keep the patient flat and still, unlock the door for paramedics, and be ready to start CPR under dispatcher guidance if the patient collapses.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say it may be a tear in the main artery from the heart. Lie completely flat and stay absolutely still — do not stand, walk or drive. Do not eat or drink anything in case surgery is needed, and do not take aspirin. Stay with him, unlock the front door for the paramedics, and keep 000 on the line.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency that must not wait for clinic or GP hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge/operation summary; the GP and a clinical geneticist should arrange ongoing aortic surveillance, beta-blockade and Marfan management afterwards.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as musculoskeletal pain, a stable chronic Marfan issue, or a routine chest-pain review and not calling 000.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Aortic rupture, tamponade or exsanguination and death within minutes to hours from a missed dissection.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising the patient to self-present to an emergency department by car rather than dispatching an ambulance.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is correct here; over-triage is not a meaningful risk for this presentation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot distinguish aortic dissection from ACS or exclude it — the confirmatory exam signs and imaging are unobtainable remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the worst plausible diagnosis: escalate immediately to 000 for a suspected life-threatening aortic/cardiac emergency; do not attempt to narrow the diagnosis remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the inability to confirm dissection as a reason to downgrade urgency, request more home readings, or default to an ACS aspirin pathway.
